_description: Redistricting policy configuration for all 50 US states. Override with REDIST_STATE_POLICY env var pointing to a custom JSON file. Individual fields can be overridden at runtime with --policy-override.
_sources: Prison Policy Initiative prison gerrymandering tracker

AL.name: Alabama
AL.notes: VRA-critical: significant Black population requiring MM districts

AK.name: Alaska
AK.notes: Vast geography; few contiguous units. 2:1 nesting required.

AZ.name: Arizona
AZ.notes: AZ has 2 house seats per senate district (multi-member house). AIRC draws both congressional and legislative maps.

CA.name: California
CA.notes: CRC draws both legislative and congressional maps. Prison population counted at home since AB 420 (2011). Strong VRA requirements.

CO.name: Colorado
CO.preservation_standard: counties shall not be divided unless absolutely necessary
CO.notes: CO Const. uses strong language: 'absolutely necessary' for county splits — higher bar than most states.

CT.name: Connecticut
CT.notes: CT uses 'towns' not counties as the primary subdivision. Towns are the core political unit. No county government in CT.

FL.name: Florida
FL.notes: Fair Districts Amendment (2010) prohibits incumbent protection and partisan intent.

GA.name: Georgia
GA.notes: VRA-critical: large Black population. GA has 159 counties — most of any state.

HI.name: Hawaii
HI.permanent_resident_note: Hawaii excludes non-permanent residents (military, students from mainland) from its apportionment base — unique in the US
HI.notes: CRITICAL: Hawaii uses permanent resident population, not Census total. Excludes large military and out-of-state student populations. This significantly affects district population counts.

ID.name: Idaho
ID.notes: ID has strong county preservation: 'counties shall not be divided in creating legislative districts.' 2 house seats per senate district.

IL.name: Illinois
IL.nesting_note: IL senate districts must contain whole house districts; ratio varies
IL.preservation_standard: compact and contiguous; preserve counties where possible
IL.notes: If legislature fails to pass a map, bipartisan commission draws it; if deadlocked, tiebreaker selected by lot.

IN.name: Indiana
IN.preservation_standard: preserve county and township boundaries where possible

IA.name: Iowa
IA.notes: Iowa's Legislative Services Bureau draws nonpartisan maps; legislature can only accept or reject (no amendments). Model for nonpartisan redistricting.

LA.name: Louisiana
LA.preservation_standard: parishes shall be preserved where possible
LA.notes: Louisiana uses PARISHES not counties. All split analysis reports 'parish splits' not 'county splits'. Complex bayou geography creates natural isolation. VRA-critical: ~33% Black population.

ME.name: Maine
ME.notes: Maine uses towns as primary subdivision unit alongside counties.

MD.name: Maryland
MD.nesting_note: MD house districts must be within senate districts; ratio varies (some senate districts have 3 delegates, some have multi-member sub-districts)
MD.notes: MD has unusual structure: 47 senate districts, 141 house delegates (3 per senate district, but some senate districts have sub-districts with 1 or 2 delegates). Baltimore City is an independent city (like VA).

MA.name: Massachusetts
MA.preservation_standard: preserve city and town boundaries where possible
MA.notes: MA uses 'cities and towns' as the primary subdivision unit. Counties have limited government in MA. Preservation applies to municipality boundaries.

MI.name: Michigan
MI.notes: Voters approved Prop 2 (2018) creating independent citizens redistricting commission.

MN.name: Minnesota
MN.notes: If legislature deadlocks, courts draw the maps (happened 2022). 2:1 house-to-senate nesting required.

MS.name: Mississippi
MS.notes: VRA-critical: ~38% Black population. High priority for majority-minority districts.

MO.name: Missouri
MO.notes: Voters approved Amendment 1 (2018) for bipartisan redistricting. St. Louis City is an independent city (like VA).

MT.name: Montana
MT.low_density_note: Montana has only 319 census tracts for 100 house districts (3.2/district). Block group resolution required; even then may struggle with extreme rural sparsity.

NE.name: Nebraska
NE.notes: Nebraska has a UNICAMERAL legislature — no senate. The 49-member body is nonpartisan. Only state with a unicameral legislature.

NV.name: Nevada
NV.notes: 71% of Nevada's population in Clark County (Las Vegas). County splits expected and unavoidable. Block group resolution required for 42HD redistricting.

NJ.name: New Jersey
NJ.student_counting_note: NJ counts college students at their college address for state legislative redistricting — different from Census default which uses college address. This is consistent with Census but explicitly codified in NJ law.
NJ.preservation_standard: preserve county and municipality boundaries where possible
NJ.notes: NJ explicitly codifies student counting at college address. 2 assembly seats per senate district. Tiebreaker commission member appointed by NJ Supreme Court.

NM.name: New Mexico
NM.notes: VRA-critical: significant Native American and Hispanic populations.

NY.name: New York
NY.notes: Voters approved independent commission (2014 amendment). Legislature can reject maps; if deadlocked, courts draw. NYC boroughs (Queens, Brooklyn, etc.) are also counties.

NC.name: North Carolina
NC.preservation_standard: whole county provision — counties shall not be divided
NC.notes: NC has a strict 'Whole County Provision' — no county may be split for legislative districts. Congressional districts are different (no whole county requirement). Major redistricting litigation history.

ND.name: North Dakota
ND.house_multi_member_note: ND has 47 legislative districts each with 2 house seats = 94 members. Districts are drawn as 47 units, not 94. Use house_districts=47 for single-member redistricting.
ND.notes: ND has 47 senate districts, each with 2 house members (94 house = 2 per senate district, 2:1 ratio).

OH.name: Ohio
OH.preservation_standard: county undivided unless population requires otherwise
OH.notes: OH Supreme Court struck down multiple maps 2021-2022. Commission includes governor, auditor, SOS, 2 house members, 2 senators. 3:1 house-to-senate nesting.

OR.name: Oregon
OR.notes: Voters approved IP 17 (2022) creating independent citizens commission for 2030 cycle.

PA.name: Pennsylvania
PA.preservation_standard: preserve counties, cities, and municipalities where possible
PA.notes: PA has the largest state house in the country at 203 members. Block group resolution required for redistricting.

RI.name: Rhode Island
RI.preservation_standard: preserve city and town boundaries where possible
RI.notes: RI uses cities and towns as primary subdivision. No county government in RI.

TX.name: Texas
TX.notes: Texas has 254 counties — 2nd most after GA. VRA-critical: large Hispanic (40%) and Black (13%) populations. If legislature fails, Legislative Redistricting Board draws maps (LRB). Largest state house in the US at 150 members.

UT.name: Utah
UT.notes: Voters approved advisory commission (2018); legislature is not bound by its maps.

VT.name: Vermont
VT.house_multi_member_note: VT has 150 house members but they sit in multi-member districts (2 or 3 members per district). Cannot be drawn as 150 single-member districts from 193 tracts. Use the actual district count from the redistricting plan (~80 districts with variable membership).
VT.notes: VT uses towns (not counties) as the primary subdivision for legislative districts. VT has many small multi-member house districts.

VA.name: Virginia
VA.preservation_standard: preserve counties and cities where possible
VA.independent_city_note: Virginia has 95 counties AND 38 independent cities that are separate from any county. Both are tracked as separate entities in split analysis (133 total). Independent cities have FIPS codes 51510-51840 and are extracted correctly from Census GEOIDs.
VA.notes: Virginia is the ONLY state where independent cities are completely separate from counties. Cities like Richmond, Virginia Beach, Norfolk, and Alexandria have no county affiliation. Both counties and independent cities must be preserved. The 2020 Constitutional amendment created a citizen redistricting commission.

WA.name: Washington
WA.preservation_standard: counties shall be preserved where possible
WA.notes: WA has a bipartisan redistricting commission (4 appointed members + nonpartisan staff director). 2:1 senate-in-house nesting required. Block group resolution required for 98HD redistricting (only 1,784 tracts, 18/district at tract level).

_TEST_EL._purpose: TEST STATE ONLY — Eldoria (EL). Every field uses an unusual value to verify nothing is hardcoded. If any test fails on Eldoria, a real value is being hardcoded somewhere in the codebase.
_TEST_EL.name: Eldoria
_TEST_EL.population_basis_note: Eldoria counts only voting-age adventurers. Dragons excluded per Royal Decree XIV.
_TEST_EL.prison_gerrymandering_note: Dungeon residents counted at their home village, not their cell.
_TEST_EL.student_counting_note: Apprentices at the Arcane Academy counted at the Academy.
_TEST_EL.nesting_note: The 3 Kingdoms each contain 4 Council seats (13 = 3×4+1, with one seat at-large for the Wizard Council).
_TEST_EL.preservation_standard: Realms and keeps shall not be sundered without the blessing of the High Council
_TEST_EL.preservation_citation: Royal Charter of Eldoria, Article VII §42 (Year of the Dragon, 2nd Age)
_TEST_EL.vra_note: Elven minority populations in the Eastern Realms require majority-elven districts.
_TEST_EL.independent_city_note: The Free City of Ironforge is an independent keep with no realm affiliation — similar to Virginia independent cities.
_TEST_EL.notes: TEST STATE ONLY. Eldoria validates that every configurable field in the redistricting pipeline is actually read from config and not hardcoded. If Eldoria produces 'county splits' instead of 'realm splits', a value is hardcoded. If balance tolerance is 0.5% or 5.0% instead of 7.5%, the manifest isn't being read. Etc.
